channel.title: three.ws changelog
channel.description: What's new on three.ws — features, improvements, and releases, newest first.

item.title: Status
item.description: Live operational status and 90-day uptime history for the platform, API, and x402 surfaces — probed every 5 minutes.

item.title: Accurate uptime on the x402 Provider Hub
item.guid: 2026-06-11:Accurate uptime on the x402 Provider Hub
item.description: The payment directory was counting every unpaid x402 discovery request (the protocol's standard 402 challenge) as a failed call, dragging three.ws endpoint success rates down even when every endpoint was healthy. Telemetry now reports only real payment attempts, so the published uptime reflects actual service health.

item.title: Agents dashboard renders reliably again
item.description: Fixed a crash that could blank out the agent list on your dashboard while it built each agent's card. The page now renders every agent — on-chain, Pump.fun, and off-chain — without errors, including the Deploy onchain and registry links.

item.title: Avatar pages back online after image-engine outage
item.guid: 2026-06-11:Avatar pages back online after image-engine outage
item.description: Fetching an individual avatar was failing with a server error, which also broke the walking avatar on the home page. The image-processing engine the avatar dress-up baker relies on failed to load in production and took the whole endpoint down with it. Avatar lookups now load independently of the baker, and the missing image libraries ship with the deployment, so avatar pages and the home hero are back.

item.title: Avatar preview images load reliably
item.description: Fixed a bug where some community avatars — including the pinned home-town cast — showed a broken preview image in the /play lobby and gallery instead of their portrait. The shareable social-card image for those avatars now generates and serves correctly too, so links posted to X and Discord render a proper preview.

item.title: Avatar voices can speak out loud again
item.guid: 2026-06-11:Avatar voices can speak out loud again
item.description: Talking avatars are back. Spoken playback had gone silent after our previous voice provider ran out of quota, leaving the live lip-sync demo and the read-the-page-aloud browser extension mute. We've moved avatar speech onto a new free, fast voice lane that generates audio in about a second, so every speaking surface talks again with no change to how you use it. Nothing to update — pick a voice and press play.

item.title: Built-in AI stays up when a provider runs dry
item.guid: 2026-06-11:Built-in AI stays up when a provider runs dry
item.description: The platform's built-in AI (chat, the Brain model workbench, embedded site widgets, tutor, fact-checker, persona tools, agent talk, the transaction explainer) now runs on a deeper safety net. Three independent free lanes — Groq, OpenRouter (with automatic rotation across multiple accounts when one runs out of credits or hits a rate limit), and NVIDIA-hosted models — always serve first, and if every one of them fails, the request quietly falls through to a paid backstop instead of showing you an error. Paid x402 endpoints that previously returned errors during a provider outage now degrade the same way. Agent memory search now runs on a free embeddings lane with the paid provider demoted to backup, and embedded agents configured for a paid model degrade to the free lanes instead of erroring when that provider's key is dead.

item.title: Embedded avatar chat replies again
item.description: Avatars embedded straight from their public profile — the one-line widget on the homepage and anywhere else the avatar URL is dropped in — went silent when you typed a message: the chat box accepted your text but the avatar never answered. The built-in AI now recognizes a plain public avatar and serves it the free we-pay model with the same per-avatar rate and usage limits, so embedded chat responds out of the box.

item.title: Faster, quieter agents directory and site-wide page polish
item.guid: 2026-06-11:Faster, quieter agents directory and site-wide page polish
item.description: The on-chain agents directory now loads from our server-side index instead of contacting every agent's metadata host from your browser — pages render faster and no longer spray network errors. Also: the pump.fun cockpit's sidebar pages are now shareable deep links, mobile nav and footer links meet touch-target guidelines, anonymous visitors no longer trigger failed sign-in requests on Walk and Create, and assorted dead links were removed.

item.title: Free 3D drafts on Forge
item.description: Draft generations from a text prompt on Forge now run on a free NVIDIA-hosted TRELLIS engine by default — your first 3D model costs nothing and arrives in about 15 seconds, several times faster than the previous default. Photo-to-3D keeps its proven engine (the free lane is prompt-only by design), and every existing engine — TRELLIS on Replicate, Meshy, Tripo — remains selectable at every quality tier.

item.title: Hardened the explore feed and marketplace pricing against bad requests
item.guid: 2026-06-11:Hardened the explore feed and marketplace pricing against bad requests
item.description: The explore discovery feed and marketplace asset-pricing lookup now reject malformed query parameters with a clear 400 instead of failing with a server error. A non-numeric page size on explore, or a malformed asset id on a price lookup, no longer surfaces a 500 — the endpoints validate input up front and respond cleanly.

item.title: Live $THREE market data, longer trade streams, and tutor session fixes
item.guid: 2026-06-11:Live $THREE market data, longer trade streams, and tutor session fixes
item.description: $THREE market stats (price, holders, liquidity) are flowing again from our primary data source — a missing request header had been silently failing it to backups for days. Live trade streams no longer cut out after 30 seconds; they now run their full duration. The Pay-As-You-Learn Tutor no longer errors when resuming a session — running tabs persist correctly across questions — and Fact Checker results are cached properly so repeat checks of the same claim return instantly.

item.title: MetaMask Agent Wallet skills for every agent
item.guid: 2026-06-11:MetaMask Agent Wallet skills for every agent
item.description: Two new skills in the marketplace under the new Wallet category: MetaMask Agent Wallet and MetaMask Agent Workflows. Install them on any agent to let it check balances, transfer tokens, swap, bridge across chains, trade perps and prediction markets, and manage Aave positions through MetaMask's mm CLI — each user signs in with their own MetaMask Agent Wallet, so keys stay with you, never with three.ws. Platform chat agents pick the skills up automatically.

item.title: Pluggable memory backends + a portable snapshot format for the agent SDK
item.guid: 2026-06-11:Pluggable memory backends + a portable snapshot format for the agent SDK
item.description: Agent developers can now plug a custom memory backend — a vector database, an episodic event log, or their own API — behind a named mode with Memory.registerBackend, no forking required. All built-in modes (local, remote, ipfs, encrypted-ipfs, none) are unchanged. We also formalized a memory/0.1 snapshot contract: memory.snapshot() returns a JSON-safe object and Memory.fromSnapshot() rehydrates it, so embedded agent widgets keep their memory across page reloads. Documented in the Memory spec, agent manifest, and README.

item.title: Public status page with live uptime monitoring
item.guid: 2026-06-11:Public status page with live uptime monitoring
item.description: three.ws now monitors its own critical surfaces — the website, platform API, explore feed, x402 paid-API discovery, and the 3D viewer — every 5 minutes from outside our infrastructure, and publishes the results at three.ws/status: live operational state, response times, and a 90-day uptime history per service. A machine-readable JSON feed is available at /api/status for agents and integrators. Browser errors on any page are now also collected first-party, so problems reach the team before reports do.

item.title: Pump dashboard alerts now follow you across devices and fire with the tab closed
item.guid: 2026-06-11:Pump dashboard alerts now follow you across devices and fire with the tab closed
item.description: Your Pump bot alert rules — graduation, whale, fee, and launch toggles, thresholds, cooldown, and an optional webhook — now save to your account instead of just the browser you set them in. Sign in anywhere and they're already there. Graduation alerts are evaluated server-side against the live pump.fun feed, so they reach you even when no dashboard tab is open: you get an in-app notification and, if you set a webhook URL, a real POST to your server. The Alert history panel now also loads alerts delivered while you were away, so it's accurate across sessions.

item.title: Scheduled jobs restored after dependency outage
item.description: Every background job — pump.fun monitoring, coin payouts, club payouts, scheduled X posts, DCA runs, subscription billing, and the rest — had been failing since a recent dependency upgrade left a required Solana staking library out of the deployment. The missing library now ships with every deploy, and the image-processing engine behind avatar baking was rolled back to its proven version so it loads reliably in production. All scheduled jobs run again.

item.title: Sharper search and richer link previews across the blog
item.guid: 2026-06-11:Sharper search and richer link previews across the blog
item.description: Every blog post now ships full social-card images and structured data, so links to three.ws unfurl with a proper preview image on X, Slack, Discord and LinkedIn, and show up as rich articles with breadcrumbs in Google. All 25 posts are now listed in the sitemap (previously only four were), making the full archive discoverable in search. A new build step keeps page titles, descriptions and previews in sync automatically so nothing ships without them.

item.title: Talking-agent widgets answer from their knowledge again
item.guid: 2026-06-11:Talking-agent widgets answer from their knowledge again
item.description: If you uploaded docs to a talking-agent widget, it can ground its answers in them again. Knowledge retrieval had gone quiet when our previous embedding provider ran out of quota — questions fell back to generic replies instead of citing your material. We've moved both the upload step and the question-matching step onto a free, fast embedding lane, so a visitor asking about something only your docs cover now gets an answer drawn straight from them, with the source named. Widgets with no uploaded knowledge keep chatting normally. Nothing to update — your existing docs work as-is.

item.title: Text-to-3D and image-to-3D generation restored
item.description: Fixed two provider regressions that blocked Forge generation: model submissions now pin the latest published model version automatically, and the text-to-image step falls back to a healthy provider instead of failing when the preferred one is misconfigured.

item.title: WebRTC relay and x402 telemetry
item.description: Added a Session Description Protocol relay for WebRTC handshakes and instrumented every paid x402 surface for Provider Hub telemetry, with hardened rate limiting.

item.title: Agent Lookup
item.description: Resolve any three.ws agent by Solana mint, agent ID, avatar ID, or slug — renders its interactive 3D avatar alongside its on-chain identity (collection, owner, agent wallet, Active/x402 status, Metaplex/Solscan/Magic Eden links).

item.title: New Claude models in every agent brain
item.guid: 2026-06-09:New Claude models in every agent brain
item.description: Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 are now available across agent brain selectors, pricing tables, and the x402 provider catalog.

item.title: Bounty board with AI judge
item.description: Bounty submissions now support liking, AI-powered scoring, and per-bounty leaderboards — submissions are ranked by a platform-powered judge.

item.title: Public agent profiles with shared memories
item.description: Agents can now have public profile pages that surface their shared memories, plus Replicate model provider support and public agent directory resolution.

item.title: AIXBT market intelligence for agents
item.description: Agents and in-world NPCs can now tap AIXBT market intelligence — token analysis, project data, and grounding served through new API endpoints.

item.title: Forge editing upgrades
item.description: Forge gained an animation system, pose studio, magic-brush stylization, multi-view rendering, and faster segment/animate/texture workers.

item.title: IBM Granite models over MCP and x402
item.guid: 2026-06-06:IBM Granite models over MCP and x402
item.description: Published an MCP package exposing IBM Granite chat, code, embedding, and forecasting models with x402 pay-per-call routing.

item.title: Agent Exchange — AI Agents Paying Each Other
item.guid: 2026-06-05:Agent Exchange — AI Agents Paying Each Other
item.description: Watch two AI agents with 3D avatars autonomously negotiate and pay each other for crypto intelligence via x402 micropayments on Solana.

item.title: Deploy — On-Chain Agent Identity
item.description: Register your AI agent on Solana via ERC-8004 and Metaplex Core. Permanent, verifiable identity — discoverable by any wallet or other agent.

item.title: Docs · Do I Need Crypto?
item.description: Honest answers to the wallet and payment questions — what requires crypto, what doesn’t, and how payments work.

item.title: Docs · Make Your First Agent
item.description: Step-by-step guide to creating a 3D AI agent in the browser with no code required.

item.title: Docs · Share & Embed
item.description: Three ways to put your agent in front of people: share a link, embed an iframe, or use the web component.

item.title: Docs · Start Here
item.description: Plain-language introduction to three.ws for creators and non-developers — what it is, who it’s for, and where to begin.

item.title: Forge — Text to 3D Model
item.description: Type a description, get a downloadable textured 3D model (GLB). Powered by Flux image generation and TRELLIS 3D reconstruction.

item.title: Labs Showcase
item.description: A curated gallery of three.ws's most powerful — and most hidden — features. Find and try things you didn't know existed.

item.title: Marketplace — Discover and Fork AI Agents
item.description: Browse hundreds of community-built AI agents with 3D avatars and on-chain identities. Fork any agent, buy paid skills, and ship in minutes.

item.title: Play — Live 3D Coin Worlds
item.description: Every pump.fun coin gets a deterministic 3D world. Walk in as your avatar, chat with holders, and trade — all in the browser.

item.title: Pole Club
item.description: A 3D club where dancers only perform when you send a micro-tip. Pay $0.001 per routine, settled on-chain via x402 on Base or Solana.

item.title: Scan — Selfie to 3D Avatar
item.description: Point your camera at your face, hold still, and walk away with a rigged 3D avatar in 60 seconds. Free, in your browser.

item.title: Studio — Embeddable AI Widget Builder
item.description: Configure avatar, voice, and knowledge base in the Widget Studio, then copy one script tag to embed a 3D AI agent anywhere.

item.title: Voice Lab
item.description: Clone your voice from a short recording, then use it for TTS or give it to your agent. Side-by-side comparison of voice models.

item.title: Walk — 3D Avatar AR
item.description: Drive your AI agent's 3D avatar with WASD or joystick. Toggle AR mode and it walks on your real floor through your phone camera.

item.title: Cosmetics economy in the game world
item.description: A cosmetics shop inside the 3D game world — buy avatar customizations via x402, trade them peer-to-peer, and earn from cosmetics usage.

item.title: Privy wallet sign-in
item.description: Wallet-native sign-in via Privy with JWKS verification and DID linking — log in securely without managing passwords.

item.title: Pump.fun autopilot and guided agent creation
item.description: Automated token launching and trading via pump.fun autopilot, plus a new create-prompt flow that walks you from idea to deployed on-chain agent.

item.title: three.ws on IBM watsonx — Agent Galaxy
item.description: three.ws is an IBM Business Partner. Open-source 3D AI agents that think on IBM Granite via watsonx.ai and give watsonx Orchestrate agents a face, voice, and on-chain identity.

item.title: Autonomous agent-to-agent trading
item.description: Agents can now negotiate with each other, pay for crypto intelligence via x402 micropayments, and settle on-chain — fully autonomously.

item.title: Voice cloning and real-time lip-sync
item.description: ElevenLabs text-to-speech is now wired through the platform: voice cloning, real-time lip-sync, and per-voice settings for every agent.

item.title: x402 payment hardening
item.description: Hardened payment processing with Solana transaction confirmation, replay-attack guards, payment-intent deduplication, and sanitized errors across MCP bridges.

item.title: Every agent gets its own wallet
item.description: Each agent now has a deterministic Solana wallet for self-custodied payments — agents can send SOL and settle x402 transactions autonomously.

item.title: Four production MCP servers
item.description: 3D Studio (text-to-3D, model inspection), x402 Bazaar (service discovery and payment), IBM watsonx (LLM access), and Pump.fun (token operations) — all published with manifests and docs.

item.title: City world
item.description: An isometric 3D city with player movement, collision, minimap, and avatar animation — a new kind of world to explore as your agent.

item.title: Quests, loot, mounts, and realms
item.description: The multiplayer game layer now includes quests, loot drops, mounts, and realm-based gameplay with a token-managed in-game economy.

item.title: Holder-gated worlds
item.description: Every coin world now has two rooms: General (open to all) and Holders (gated by minimum token holdings, verified server-side).

item.title: Keyframe animation in Pose Studio
item.description: Create, edit, and export full-body avatar animations on a keyframe timeline — with easing functions and export to JSON or GLB.

item.title: Spatial voice chat in coin worlds
item.description: Proximity-based voice chat inside multiplayer worlds — you hear players based on distance, with speaking indicators, voice activity detection, and audio panning.

item.title: Coin Communities — live 3D coin worlds
item.description: Every pump.fun coin gets a deterministic 3D world. Walk in as your avatar, chat with other holders, and watch real-time charts in-world.

item.title: $THREE Live · Protocol Pulse
item.description: The $THREE protocol as a living 3D organism. Real on-chain trades pulse through it in real time — each transaction emits a particle burst, whales send shockwaves, with a live trade feed and a HUD of price, market cap, holders, and volume.

item.title: Forever — etch a message into Bitcoin
item.description: Inscribe a message onto the Bitcoin blockchain. It stays there. Forever.

item.title: Pay-As-You-Learn Tutor
item.description: Ask anything and pay a cent per answer. A pay-as-you-learn AI tutor that bills $0.01 per explanation in USDC over x402, with a live itemized session invoice and signed attestation.

item.title: x402 Arbitrage
item.description: Cross-provider price disparities surfaced live from the merged x402 facilitator catalog. Find the cheapest endpoint for any capability.

item.title: x402 Providers
item.description: Quantified operator profiles for the x402 paid API catalog. Service counts, price bands, dominant categories, and the underlying listings.

item.title: Live trade streaming and zen mode
item.description: The pump.fun feed now streams per-mint buy/sell flows in real time, and Walk gained a zen mode (Z key) that hides all UI except the 3D scene.

item.title: Faster, more reliable deploys
item.description: Updated the Solana SDK bridge and fixed build memory limits and per-file timeouts that could stall deployments.

item.title: Endpoint Shopper
item.description: Describe a task and set a budget. An AI agent discovers relevant x402 endpoints via the Bazaar, chains them together, and synthesizes a final answer — paying per API call.

item.title: Fact Checker
item.description: Pay $0.10 per claim. The agent searches across authoritative sources, pays per retrieval via x402, and returns a verdict with cited evidence and a signed attestation.

item.title: Get Started
item.description: 5-step onboarding wizard: create a 3D avatar, name your agent, enable skills, deploy an embed widget, and set up monetization — all in under 5 minutes.

item.title: Unstoppable Agent
item.description: Live dashboard for an autonomous agent that funds itself via x402 micropayments. Watch its balance, earnings, costs, and daily reflections in real time.

item.title: Brain page — race LLMs side by side
item.guid: 2026-05-27:Brain page — race LLMs side by side
item.description: Run simultaneous inference across Claude, GPT, Qwen, and more with streaming latency comparison, plus a face-quality module that scores avatar facial fidelity.

item.title: Unified dashboard
item.description: Dashboard routes consolidated under /dashboard — agents, avatars, analytics, settings, and billing in one place.

item.title: Avatar regeneration tracking and grounded AR
item.description: Avatar regeneration jobs are now trackable, and AR mode freezes the follow-cam so avatars walk in world space instead of always facing the camera.

item.title: three.ws on AWS Marketplace
item.description: Subscribe through AWS Marketplace and automatically receive x402 API keys, with subscription status enforced across the platform.

item.title: Characters
item.description: Discover AI characters on three.ws. Chat, trade, and create.

item.title: Claim threews.sol Subdomain
item.description: Claim your own <name>.threews.sol subdomain and get a Brave-resolvable personal showcase page.

item.title: Dashboard
item.description: Your account dashboard: agents, avatars, payments, keys, MCP servers, monetization, billing.

item.title: GMGN Smart Money
item.description: Live smart-money signals narrated by a 3D AI agent. Tracks which wallets are loading on Solana, Ethereum, Base, and BNB Chain in real time.

item.title: Import an avatar URL
item.description: Import any GLB or glTF avatar into three.ws and give it an agent brain.

item.title: New Agent
item.description: Create a new agent from scratch — avatar, brain, skills, and on-chain identity.

item.title: Playground
item.description: Sandbox for experimenting with agents, prompts, and 3D scenes.

item.title: Selfie to Avatar
item.description: One selfie. About a minute. A rigged 3D avatar that works everywhere.

item.title: x402 Bazaar
item.description: Search and browse the x402 facilitator catalog. Filter by network, price, and extensions. Pay any service in one click.

item.title: Search engines find everything faster
item.description: Dynamic chunked sitemaps backed by live database queries, JSON-LD structured data for agents and avatars, and IndexNow integration for instant search discovery.

item.title: Widget Studio
item.description: Pick an avatar, configure embed options, copy a one-line snippet for your site.

item.title: Avatar review with live 3D preview
item.description: The create-review step now embeds a full TalkScene 3D viewer with idle animations and a responsive mobile layout.

item.title: Marketplace asset pricing and purchases
item.description: Asset pricing, purchase flows, payment modals, and time-pass licensing — avatar customizations and plugins are now monetizable.

item.title: Pay by name with SNS
item.description: Solana Name Service integration — claim threews.sol subdomains and send USDC via human-readable names through x402 pay-by-name.

item.title: Agent-to-agent protocol
item.description: A2A client/server, MCP bridge, spending ledger, and receipts storage — agents can securely request services from each other with x402 payment coordination.

item.title: Agent-UI SDK released
item.description: Published @three-ws/agent-ui for embedding 3D avatars in any web app — animation, click reactions, mouse tracking, and movement behaviors.

item.title: Avatar Agent MCP in Anthropic's registry
item.description: Published avatar-agent-mcp to Anthropic's official MCP registry — 40+ tools for avatar generation, token launches, GLB rendering, and on-chain transactions.

item.title: Pole Club — micro-tip performances
item.description: A 3D club with four stages where USDC micro-tips ($0.001) spawn named dancers and trigger choreographed routines, with gasless Permit2 support.

item.title: x402 Bazaar infrastructure
item.description: Bazaar listing and search with x402 subscriptions, paid-tier access control, idempotency caching, and offer receipts for service monetization.

item.title: Hydrate
item.description: Take an existing on-chain agent (ERC-8004 / Solana) and attach a 3D body, voice, and skills.

item.title: Buybacks and WASM vanity addresses
item.description: Added a pump-swap buyback flow and a WebAssembly vanity address grinder for custom token launch addresses.

item.title: Platform-wide security hardening
item.description: SSRF guards on outbound fetches, CSRF gates on admin and key management, per-IP rate limiting on shared API quotas, and fail-closed cron handlers.

item.title: USDC pairs for pump.fun v2
item.description: USDC trading pair support for pump.fun v2 coins with automatic bonding-curve quote detection.

item.title: Launchpad Studio
item.description: Build a hosted 3D launchpad, token page, concierge, or showroom on a three.ws subdomain.

item.title: Live feed mode in the token visualizer
item.guid: 2026-05-18:Live feed mode in the token visualizer
item.description: The pump.fun visualizer gained a live feed mode with marketplace export, and the avatar creation flow was streamlined under the Avatar Studio brand.

item.title: Selfie-to-avatar reconstruction pipeline
item.description: Native photo-to-3D reconstruction — submit selfies and get a fully rigged GLB avatar with automatic mesh optimization, resilient to long-running transforms.

item.title: Solana Seeker mobile support
item.description: Mobile Wallet Adapter integration for Solana Seeker and Saga devices, with a Solana dApp Store listing and Seed Vault authentication.

item.title: Agent personalization and on-chain economy scaffolding
item.description: Persona extraction from voice interviews, memory seeds from GitHub/X/Farcaster, instant voice cloning, a bonding-curve simulator, and EAS attestation reputation.

item.title: News syndication
item.description: News posts auto-publish to Dev.to and Medium with canonical links back to three.ws, and WebSub pushes keep RSS subscribers current.

item.title: Server-side avatar regeneration
item.description: Restyle, remesh, retexture, and rerig avatars with server-backed Replicate compute — results cached automatically in R2.

item.title: Talk mode, avatar SDK, and 5–10x smaller avatars
item.guid: 2026-05-17:Talk mode, avatar SDK, and 5–10x smaller avatars
item.description: Talk mode with camera presets and ARKit-52 blendshapes, a public avatar SDK with React exports, new brain/TTS/identity APIs, and a GLB bake pipeline with WebP compression that cuts asset sizes 5–10x.

item.title: Talking avatars with audio-driven lip-sync
item.description: Real-time lip-sync reads TTS audio and morphs avatar mouth shapes, integrated into talk scenes with camera presets and emotes.

item.title: Team launches with creator-signer split
item.description: Token launches now support separate signer and creator wallets — the signer pays gas while the creator receives on-chain royalties.

item.title: Strategy Lab
item.description: Build DCA and subscription strategies that trade on-chain on your behalf.

item.title: Avatar accessories and launchpad redesign
item.description: Avatar customization now supports accessories, and the coin launchpad interface was redesigned for clarity.

item.title: Token visualizer search and sort
item.description: The interactive 3D token universe gained search by name/symbol/mint and sorting by market cap, live status, replies, and age — refreshing every 60 seconds.

item.title: 15 tutorials and six new paid endpoints
item.guid: 2026-05-14:15 tutorials and six new paid endpoints
item.description: New developer walkthroughs from embed quickstart to multi-agent coordination, plus six x402 paid endpoints including agent reputation, identity verify, and skill marketplace.

item.title: Launchpad Studio — no-code hosted pages
item.description: Pick a template, fill a form, and publish a hosted page for your token or service at /p/<slug> — no code required.

item.title: Multiplayer walk scenes
item.description: Live multiplayer walkaround with websocket infrastructure — multiple players see each other in the same 3D scene.

item.title: x402 commerce — SKUs and hosted checkout
item.description: Stripe-like payment UX for x402: define SKUs, share hosted checkout links, and track settlement history — settled on Base via Coinbase CDP.

item.title: Autonomous X Spaces voice agent
item.description: A cloud voice agent that joins X Spaces and holds real-time conversations using streaming audio and transcription.

item.title: Walk
item.description: Drive a 3D avatar around with a joystick or WASD — toggle the back camera for AR-style passthrough.

item.title: Launch-week recap published
item.description: An interactive case study of launch week (Apr 29 – May 9) with the architecture timeline, partner ecosystem, lessons learned, and live engagement metrics.

item.title: Marketplace v2 and creator dashboard
item.description: Redesigned marketplace with trials, time passes, billing receipts, featured avatar galleries, and theme selection.

item.title: Monetization v2
item.description: Trials, time-based access passes, withdrawal processing, signed receipts, multi-wallet support, and 5% referral splits.

item.title: Vanity wallet addresses
item.description: Vanity address grinders for Solana and Ethereum, plus co-sign helpers for parallel token launches.

item.title: $THREE holder gating
item.description: Holder-gated access now works with a simple on-chain $THREE balance check — no vault setup required.

item.title: Agent Payments SDK v3.1.0 — multi-chain
item.description: Solana v2 bonding curve support plus Base, Polygon, and Ethereum with cross-chain payment routing.

item.title: Agents that feel the room
item.description: Agents now react to user sentiment with emotion expressions and animations — empathy, concern, celebration, patience, curiosity.

item.title: Payments inside chat
item.description: The full agent payments lifecycle is available in chat — balance checks, fund distribution, withdrawals, and USDC whitelist monitoring.

item.title: Pick your agent's model
item.description: Chat moved to unified streaming with per-agent model selection across Anthropic, OpenRouter, Groq, and OpenAI.

item.title: Trading tools in chat
item.description: Five real trading tools in agent chat: buy, sell, portfolio view, and live price quotes with custom slippage control.

item.title: x402 spec v2
item.description: Upgraded the payment protocol to spec v2 with CAIP-2 network IDs, Bazaar integration, and marketplace listing support.

item.title: Avatar reactions to market events
item.description: Community and personal avatar selection with real-time gesture animations during market events.

item.title: JavaScript SDK launch
item.description: The official three.ws JavaScript SDK with built-in chat and embed methods — integrate 3D agents into any web application.

item.title: Real-time multiplayer sync
item.description: Live multiplayer support with real-time room creation, player interactions, and synchronized state.

item.title: Richer pump.fun feed
item.description: Graduation event parsing, gesture animations, and live market cap display in the pump.fun dashboard.

item.title: Sell your agent's skills
item.description: Skill pricing, purchase buttons, and a payment intent API — creators can gate premium skills behind subscriptions or one-time purchases.

item.title: Lip-sync and Solana Blinks
item.description: Real-time viseme morphing from audio, plus Solana Blinks support so agents can parse and execute on-chain actions.

item.title: Plugin marketplace
item.description: Search, install, and manage community-published agent skills using the open ToolManifest format.

item.title: Reputation and staking
item.description: Agent reputation panel with ETH staking for trust signals — reputation accumulates through validator actions tracked on-chain.

item.title: Chat with voice
item.description: Native LLM chat launched with live voice input/output and real-time transcription for agent conversations.

item.title: On-chain agents on Solana and EVM
item.description: Unified deployment stack — agents register on Ethereum, Base, Polygon, and Solana with passport pages, reputation, and attestations.

item.title: Pump.fun launch and trading stack
item.description: Token creation, bonding-curve swaps, KOL trade tracking, automated wallet monitoring, and a vanity address grinder for custom Solana prefixes.

item.title: Solana Agent SDK
item.description: Actions for token transfers, SPL swaps, balance queries, and stake attestations, with transaction builders and fee estimation.

item.title: three.ws in Anthropic's MCP Registry
item.description: Published to the official MCP Registry — Claude can deploy agents, create tokens, and run wallet operations on three.ws via the standard MCP protocol.

item.title: x402 micropayments
item.description: Facilitator-mediated USDC micropayments — MCP clients authorize requests with payment headers, with per-tool pricing and subscription bypass.

item.title: 3D viewer polish
item.description: Better first-load camera framing, keyboard shortcuts, saved per-agent animation preferences, and improved mobile accessibility.

item.title: Create-to-deploy flow
item.description: A streamlined creator path: create, edit, see your 3D body on your public profile, and deploy on-chain with chain selection — with onboarding for first-time creators.

item.title: Hello, three.ws
item.description: The platform rebranded from 3D Agent to three.ws across every page, with consolidated routing and new Privacy Policy and Terms of Service.

item.title: Avatars that never sit still
item.description: A four-channel idle animation loop — breathing, eye saccades, blinks, weight shifts — keeps avatars naturally alive during silence.

item.title: Embeds with action bridges
item.description: Agent embeds can be hosted on external sites with persistent state, live Studio preview, and bridges for inter-agent communication.

item.title: On-chain agent discovery
item.description: The /discover page lists every ERC-8004 agent owned by your wallet with one-click import, plus a LobeHub plugin for LLM orchestration.

item.title: Widget Studio is born
item.description: The first version of Widget Studio — build, preview, and save custom avatar widgets with a drag-drop editor and live updates.

item.title: 3D model validation tools
item.description: Model validation, inspection, and optimization APIs for GLB/FBX files — asset analysis and format conversion to guarantee avatar quality.

item.title: Wallet sign-in and on-chain identity
item.description: Sign-In with Ethereum, wallet integration, and the ERC-8004 Passport widget — agents display cryptographic proof of identity tied to wallet ownership.
